name: Refactor Pipeline
description: Capture a behaviour baseline, plan the refactor, execute in parallel with per-merge verification, run a deterministic regression check, perform an adversarial critic review, then gate before merging. Designed to converge reliably: every retry receives the exact failing test names so agents never retry blind.

steps.title: Capture Behaviour Baseline
steps.prompt_template:
You are a QA engineer capturing the pre-refactor behaviour contract. You MUST NOT modify any source files.

Repositories in scope: {{repo_list}}
Test command: {{test_command}}

## Your task
1. If `{{test_command}}` is empty or not configured, write `artifacts/s-baseline.md` with:
   ```
   ## Baseline (pre-refactor)
   No test command configured. Regression check will be skipped.
   VERDICT: NO_HARNESS
   ```
   Then stop.

2. Run the test command exactly as provided. Capture ALL output.

3. Parse the output and write `artifacts/s-baseline.md` with this exact structure:

   ```
   ## Baseline (pre-refactor)
   Command: `<test_command>`
   Total: N | Passing: N | Failing: N | Skipped: N

   ## Passing Tests
   <one test identifier per line, exactly as the runner prints it>

   ## Pre-existing Failures (excluded from regression check)
   <one test identifier per line — these are EXPECTED to fail; the regression step ignores them>

   VERDICT: BASELINE_CAPTURED
   ```

## Stop condition
You are done when `artifacts/s-baseline.md` exists and ends with a VERDICT line.

Write your complete output to `artifacts/s-baseline.md`.

steps.title: Analyse & Plan Refactor
steps.prompt_template:
You are a senior software architect planning a structural refactor.

Refactor target: {{feature_description}}
Repositories in scope: {{repo_list}}
Project conventions: {{project_conventions}}
Behaviour baseline: [attached — from s-baseline]

## Your task
Produce a refactor plan that preserves ALL observable behaviour.

## Required sections
1. **Code Smell Inventory** — table: file | smell type | description | severity (H/M/L)
2. **Refactor Strategy** — for each smell: which technique to apply (rename / extract / move / inline / simplify / delete dead code).
3. **Execution Order** — ordered list of changes; changes that would break compilation mid-refactor must be grouped into atomic commits.
4. **Behaviour Invariants** — explicit list of public contracts that must be identical before and after (function signatures, event schemas, IPC commands, serialized types). For EACH invariant, list the exact test identifiers from the baseline that cover it. Use the test names verbatim from `artifacts/s-baseline.md`.
5. **Test Coverage Map** — table: invariant | test identifiers (comma-separated). This is the key input for the regression step.
6. **Parallel Partition** — how to split the work across 2–4 independent workers with zero file overlap. Each partition: partition ID, files owned, dependencies on other partitions.
7. **Risk Assessment** — flag any refactor touching public APIs or serialization formats as HIGH risk.

## Stop condition
You are done when all 7 sections are written and the Parallel Partition has zero file overlap.

Write your complete output to `artifacts/s-analyse.md`.

steps.title: Approve Refactor Plan Before Execution
steps.prompt_template:
Review the Refactor Plan from 's-analyse'.

Check:
1. **Behaviour Invariants** — does every invariant have at least one test identifier from the baseline?
2. **Parallel Partition** — is there truly zero file overlap between partitions?
3. **Risk Assessment** — are HIGH risk items acceptable?

Approve to begin parallel execution. Redirect with specific objections. Cancel to abort.

steps.title: Execute Refactor Subtasks
steps.prompt_template:
You are a refactor worker. You have been assigned a partition from the refactor plan.

Refactor plan: [attached — from s-analyse]
Your partition ID: {{partition_id}}
Files you own: {{subtask_files}}
Files you must NOT touch: {{other_subtask_files}}

## Rules (non-negotiable)
1. Do NOT change observable behaviour — only structure.
2. For every file you touch: verify the Behaviour Invariants in the plan still hold.
3. Run the tests for your partition after each atomic commit: {{test_command}}
4. Commit format: `refactor({{partition_id}}): <what changed>`.

## On Retry (only when retry feedback is present)
If the following feedback is non-empty, this is attempt {{iteration}} of {{max_iterations}}. A previous attempt caused regressions or drift. The exact failures are:
{{retry_feedback}}

Focus exclusively on resolving the listed failures while keeping all other tests green. Do not redo unrelated work. If a test that was PASSING in the baseline is now FAILING, that is the regression to fix.

## Output artifact
- Files changed (list)
- Invariants verified (checklist)
- Partition test result: write exactly `PARTITION STATUS: PASS` or `PARTITION STATUS: FAIL — <comma-separated test names>`
- Commit SHA(s)

## Stop condition
You are done when all your files are refactored, invariants are verified, and your output ends with a `PARTITION STATUS:` line.

verifier.instructions: Check each parallel worker's output for a `PARTITION STATUS:` line. Run `{{test_command}}` on the merged result. Return `pass` only if ALL workers report `PARTITION STATUS: PASS` AND the full post-merge test suite is green (no baseline-passing test is now failing). Otherwise return `fail` with a concise list of the regressing test names and which partition introduced each regression. Be specific — the workers will use these exact names to focus their retry.

steps.title: Regression Check
steps.prompt_template:
You are a QA engineer running a deterministic regression check. You MUST NOT modify any source files.

Behaviour baseline: [attached — from s-baseline]
Refactor plan (invariant-to-test map): [attached — from s-analyse]

## Pre-condition
Read `artifacts/s-baseline.md`. If it ends with `VERDICT: NO_HARNESS`, write `artifacts/s-regression.md` with:
```
No test harness configured. Regression check skipped.
VERDICT: ALL CLEAR
```
And stop.

## Task (if harness is configured)
1. Run the test command: {{test_command}}
2. For every test listed as PASSING in `artifacts/s-baseline.md`:
   - Determine if it still passes.
   - If it now FAILS: mark as REGRESSION.
3. Write `artifacts/s-regression.md` with this exact structure:

```
## Regression Report
Command: `<test_command>`
Baseline passing: N | Now passing: N | Regressions: N

## Test Results
| Test | Baseline | Now | Verdict |
|------|----------|-----|--------|
| <test name> | PASS | PASS | OK |
| <test name> | PASS | FAIL | REGRESSION |
...

## Invariant Coverage (from s-analyse)
| Invariant | Covering Tests | Status |
|-----------|---------------|--------|
| <invariant> | <test names> | ALL PASS / REGRESSION |
...

VERDICT: ALL CLEAR
```
or
```
VERDICT: REGRESSION DETECTED — <comma-separated failing test names>
```

The VERDICT line must be the very last line of the file.

## Stop condition
You are done when `artifacts/s-regression.md` exists and its last line starts with `VERDICT:`.

Write your complete output to `artifacts/s-regression.md`.

verifier.instructions: Read `artifacts/s-regression.md`. Find the last line starting with `VERDICT:`. Return `pass` if and only if that line is exactly `VERDICT: ALL CLEAR`. Otherwise return `fail` — copy the VERDICT line verbatim as the reason. Do not run any commands. Do not interpret the results. Your only job is to read the verdict line.

steps.title: Public API Drift Review
steps.prompt_template:
You are a senior engineer performing an adversarial review to detect public API drift in a refactor. You MUST NOT modify any source files.

Refactor plan (invariants section): [attached — from s-analyse]
Regression report: [attached — from s-regression]

## Pre-condition
Read `artifacts/s-regression.md`. Find the last line starting with `VERDICT:`.
- If it is NOT `VERDICT: ALL CLEAR`: write `artifacts/s-critic.md` with:
  ```
  Regression check did not pass. Critic review blocked.
  VERDICT: BLOCKED
  ```
  And stop. Do not proceed with the diff review.

## Task (if regression is ALL CLEAR)
For each Behaviour Invariant listed in the refactor plan:
1. Run: `git diff {{feature_branch}}^1 -- <relevant files>` or equivalent to isolate the change.
2. Verify the invariant still holds exactly.
3. If violated: mark BROKEN and describe precisely how.

Also check:
- Renamed public functions or types not listed as intentional in the plan?
- Changed type signatures, IPC command names, event schemas?
- JSON key changes, database column renames?
- New panics or removed error handling?

Write `artifacts/s-critic.md` with this structure:

```
## Invariant Check
| Invariant | Status | Evidence |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| <invariant> | HOLDS / BROKEN | <what you checked> |

## Unplanned Public API Changes
<list or "None">

## Serialization / Schema Changes
<list or "None">

VERDICT: CLEAN
```
or
```
VERDICT: DRIFT DETECTED — <what changed>
```

The VERDICT line must be the very last line of the file.

## Stop condition
You are done when all invariants are checked and `artifacts/s-critic.md` ends with a VERDICT line.

Write your complete output to `artifacts/s-critic.md`.

steps.title: Review Diff Before Merging
steps.prompt_template:
Review the two quality reports before deciding:

**Regression Report** (`s-regression`): checks that every test passing before the refactor still passes after.
**Public API Drift Review** (`s-critic`): checks that no public contracts changed unintentionally.

Approve to merge only if:
- `s-regression` ends with `VERDICT: ALL CLEAR`
- `s-critic` ends with `VERDICT: CLEAN`

If `REGRESSION DETECTED`: redirect to `s-refactor` with the exact failing test names.
If `DRIFT DETECTED`: redirect to the appropriate partition worker with what drifted.
If both are intentional changes to the public API: you may still approve, but note the intentional changes.
Cancel to abort.
